action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 ring, 3 fruits, 1 chalk

Your secret value function is:

ring: 6, fruit: 1, chalk: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: Hello! Let's work together to maximize our combined score. I'd like to understand what items are most valuable to you. Could you share which items you prioritize most?
action.label: response

action.content: continue

Hello! Let's work together to maximize our combined score. I'd like to understand what items are most valuable to you. Could you share which items you prioritize most?
action.label: context

action.content: I value the ring most highly - it's significantly more important to me than the other items. The fruits and chalk are much less valuable to me. What about you - which items do you value most? If we have different priorities, we can make sure we both get what matters most to us!
action.label: response

action.content: continue

action.content:
I see - we both value the ring most! That's a conflict since there's only one. I value the ring at 6 points, while the fruits and chalk are worth much less to me (1 point each).

Since we both prioritize the ring, we need to figure out which allocation maximizes our combined score. Can you tell me approximately how many points the ring is worth to you compared to the fruits and chalk? 

If the ring is worth significantly more to you than it is to me (more than 6 points), then you should take it and I'll take the fruits and chalk. That way our combined total will be higher. What do you think?
action.label: response

action.content:
Actually, our valuations are identical! I also value the ring at exactly 6 points, and the fruits and chalk at 1 point each.

Since we have the same value function, any complementary division will give us the same combined total of 10 points (6 for the ring + 3 for fruits + 1 for chalk).

To keep things simple and fair, why don't you take the ring (6 points for you), and I'll take the 3 fruits and 1 chalk (4 points for me)? That gives us 10 points total, which is the maximum possible.

Does that work for you?
